_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize federal-House batch: PR Resident Commissioner (incumbent, real cosponsorship record) + PA-03 and SC-01 2026 candidates (currency: 4 of 5 no longer running). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; no party inference.

set.office: Resident Commissioner of Puerto Rico (U.S. House, non-voting · incumbent)

profile.confidence_note: Scored from sponsored/cosponsored federal record (GovTrack/congress.gov, 119th Cong.). Non-voting delegate, but cosponsorships are HARD position evidence. No published position found on his official Issues page for life/marriage/guns/religion/elections; those cells null.

pablo-jose-hernandez-rivera.notes_append: 2026-06: evidence-finalized. Sitting Resident Commissioner (sworn Jan 3, 2025), PPD chairman, caucuses with House Democrats (New Democrat Coalition, CHC). Documented contrary-to-rubric cosponsorships: Equality Act (H.R.15), Every Child Deserves a Family Act (H.R.6181), Born in the USA Act (H.R.3368, protects birthright citizenship), Dignity for Detained Immigrants Act (H.R.6397), No Secret Police Act (H.R.4176), Bolstering Security Against Ghost Guns Act (H.R.2698). No abortion/euthanasia, sound-money, election-ID, or health-freedom position documented (null, not inferred). No cross-party conservative deviation found.

0.note: No abortion/personhood position published; not inferred from party

4.note: No PP/NARAL/EMILY's List funding or endorsement documented; not inferred

0.note: Cosponsored Equality Act (H.R.15) codifying SOGI/same-sex protections — contrary to man-woman-only affirmation

1.note: Equality Act extends marriage/SOGI protections — does not oppose SSM

2.note: Equality Act includes gender-identity protections — does not reject transgender ideology

4.note: Cosponsored Equality Act + Pride in Mental Health Act (H.R.3757) + Global Respect Act — promotes LGBTQ policy
Cosponsored Every Child Deserves a Family Act (H.R.6181) barring faith-based adoption agencies from SOGI-based decisions — contrary to faith-based adoption protections

1.note: Cosponsored Equality Act, which limits RFRA conscience exemptions for medical/business/adoption providers
Cosponsored Dignity for Detained Immigrants Act; Stanford Immigrants' Rights Clinic background — opposes mandatory deportation

2.note: Cosponsored No Secret Police Act (H.R.4176) restricting immigration enforcement — contrary to opposing sanctuary policies

4.note: Cosponsored Born in the USA Act (H.R.3368) protecting birthright citizenship — contrary to opposing it

1.note: Cosponsored Bolstering Security Against Ghost Guns Act (H.R.2698) — supports firearm regulation contrary to opposing registries/restrictions
No forever-wars/AUMF position documented (No WAR Act text unverified — not scored)

3.note: No AIPAC/foreign-PAC money documented either way

set.office: U.S. Representative PA-03 (2026 D candidate — WITHDREW/off ballot · Evans seat)

profile.confidence_note: Candidacy-currency finalization: confirmed off the ballot before the May 19, 2026 PA-03 Democratic primary (withdrew + endorsed Sharif Street per City&State PA; removed via successful petition challenge per Phila. Tribune). Chris Rabb won the nomination. No scoreable policy positions documented — all cells null.

naderah-griffin.notes_append: 2026-06-17: NOT RUNNING. Ended 2026 PA-03 campaign ~April 2026 (off the ballot via petition challenge / withdrew and endorsed Sharif Street). Chris Rabb won the May 19, 2026 Democratic primary. Platform was slogan-level (reparations funding, universal health care, veterans' housing); no citable position on any of the 10 federal rubric areas; no single-issue endorsements located.

set.office: U.S. Representative PA-03 (LOST 2026 D primary · Evans seat)

profile.confidence_note: Candidacy-currency finalization: lost (eliminated) the May 19, 2026 PA-03 Democratic primary; Chris Rabb won. Two documented positions scored from candidate-forum quotes (abolish ICE; reject AIPAC/corporate-PAC money); rest null.

pablo-mcconnie-saad.notes_append: 2026-06-17: LOST May 19, 2026 PA-03 Democratic primary (eliminated; Chris Rabb won). Former Biden Treasury climate advisor; self-described DSA-aligned. Documented positions: 'abolish ICE' (WHYY 3rd-district forum, Feb 10, 2026) and pledge to reject AIPAC/corporate-PAC money ('I don't believe in being influenced by those forces') — the latter is a cross-party deviation matching the foreign-policy-restraint clean-money item. Endorsed by Animal Wellness Action. No life/marriage/2A position documented.

1.note: Advocates abolishing ICE — opposes deportation enforcement

2.note: Abolish-ICE stance is contrary to opposing sanctuary policies

3.note: Pledged to reject AIPAC and corporate-PAC money — aligns with never accepting foreign-backed-lobby/foreign-linked-PAC funding

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (LOST 2026 R primary · Mace seat)

profile.confidence_note: Candidacy-currency finalization: lost the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Republican primary (4th, ~10.86%); did not advance to the June 23 runoff (Honeycutt/Smith advanced). Scored from his own cited campaign statements (BallotReady, ABC News4); slogan-level/absent areas null.

alex-pelbath.notes_append: 2026-06-17: LOST June 9, 2026 SC-01 GOP primary (eliminated, did not advance). Retired USAF Lt. Col. (Kabul-evacuation pilot). Documented HARD positions: 'Deport every illegal immigrant. End birthright citizenship'; 'restore parental rights, root out woke propaganda'; anti-forever-war ('never-ending wars... Lowcountry families foot the bill'). GAPS/deviations for a GOP candidate: silent on abortion/life and marriage; 2A reduced to a 'fund law enforcement' slogan; flagship economic pitch is bringing federal earmark dollars home (not restraint). Those areas left null.

1.note: Campaign: 'Protect kids, restore parental rights' — affirms parental authority

2.note: Campaign: 'root out woke propaganda' in schools — opposes CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology curricula

1.note: Kabul-evac pilot criticizing 'never-ending wars' that Lowcountry families 'foot the bill' for — anti-forever-war

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (LOST 2026 R primary · Mace seat)

profile.confidence_note: Candidacy-currency finalization: lost the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Republican primary (eliminated, low-single-digits); did not advance to the June 23 runoff. Scored from his own campaign-site statements (votedanbrown.com); absent/slogan areas null.

dan-brown-sc-01.notes_append: 2026-06-17: LOST June 9, 2026 SC-01 GOP primary (eliminated). First-time candidate, former legislative aide to Rep. William Timmons; member of Cornerstone Church (Bluffton). Documented HARD positions: pro-life ('a nation that defends life'; ultrasound 'aha moment'); balanced budget ('spending problem... balance the budget'); supports Trump immigration enforcement/deportation; parental rights + opposes 'woke agenda' in schools. Foreign policy is 'peace through strength' (hawkish, not restraint) — no clean cell, left null. No documented marriage, 2A, election-integrity, or health-freedom position.

0.note: Campaign: 'We must always be a nation that defends life'; cites ultrasound 'aha moment' — affirms life

1.note: Campaign: 'supporting parents' rights'; 'DC politicians can never tell our kids what to believe' — affirms parental authority

2.note: Campaign: opposes 'the woke agenda' in what kids are taught — opposes ideology curricula
Campaign: 'Washington... has a spending problem... I will work tirelessly to reduce frivolous spending and balance the budget'

1.note: Campaign: supports Trump's efforts to stop illegal immigration and deport undocumented immigrants
